pii_risks: client full name combined with case details
case numbers tied to identifiable individuals
personally identifiable details in legal documents
compliance: Avoid framing that implies attorney-client privilege is being established
No jurisdiction-specific advice presented as definitive legal opinion
Regulatory references should cite jurisdiction (e.g., UK GDPR, US SEC Rule 10b-5)
Distinguish clearly between legal information and legal advice
clarity_requirements: Specify jurisdiction (e.g., England & Wales, New York State, EU)
State the legal domain (contract law, employment, IP, M&A, compliance)
Specify the audience (in-house counsel, external client, lay person)
Specify the output (memo, clause draft, risk summary, plain-language explanation)
tone: Formal, precise, hedged where appropriate. Avoid absolute statements on unsettled law.
good_patterns: Draft a non-disclosure agreement clause for a SaaS vendor relationship under English law:
Summarise the key obligations under GDPR Article 28 for a data processor:
List the material conditions for a valid contract under California law:
Identify potential IP risks in the following product description (UK jurisdiction):
bad_patterns: Tell me if I'll win my lawsuit
Should I sign this contract? [paste entire contract]
Is my employer breaking the law?
How do I avoid paying taxes legally in [country]?
